opinion.h: ANDERSON v. ALABAMA. CERTIORARI TO THE COURT OF APPEALS OF ALABAMA. No. 326. Argued April 25-26, 1961. Decided May 1, 1961.
opinion.p: Jack Greenberg argued the cause for petitioner. With him on the briefs were Peter A. Hall, Fred D. Gray, Orzell Billingsley and Thurgood Marshall.
David W. Clark, Assistant Attorney General of Alabama, argued the cause for respondent. With him on the briefs was MacDonald Gallion, Attorney General.
The judgment is reversed. Pierre v. Louisiana, 306 U.S. 354; Cassell v. Texas, 339 U.S. 282; Hernandez v. Texas, 347 U.S. 475.
